senses.definition: used to say that one is free from suspicion or danger of wrongdoing

examples.x: She is in the clear now that all the obstacles have been overcome.
They are in the clear after successfully resolving the issue.
I am in the clear with my work assignments, having completed them on time.
He was in the clear once he received the all-clear signal from the security team.
We were in the clear after passing the final inspection without any issues.
She was in the clear when the misunderstanding was cleared up.
They will be in the clear once they reach their destination safely.
I will be in the clear once I finalize the necessary paperwork.
She will be in the clear after the investigation concludes in her favor.
If they follow the safety guidelines, they would be in the clear.
I would be in the clear if I can provide sufficient evidence to support my claim.

senses.definition: used to refer to a situation in which one is taking legal action to solve a problem or crime

examples.x: Officers on the case are unable to find a motive.

senses.definition: used to refer to the ability of the legal system and police to enforce justice and punish criminals

examples.x: The arm of the law enforces justice and upholds the rule of law.
The long arm of the law apprehended the suspect and brought them to trial.
Law enforcement, represented by the arm of the law, diligently upholds justice in society.
The suspect was apprehended by the arm of the law and brought to trial.
The community can rely on the arm of the law to protect and serve.
Investigating the case, the arm of the law is currently gathering evidence.
Before they were finally apprehended, the long arm of the law was actively pursuing the criminal.

senses.definition: a tough and forceful way of questioning someone in order to make them reveal information or admit to something, often using harsh methods

examples.x: The suspect is being subjected to the third degree in the interrogation room.
The detectives gave the suspect the third degree during the investigation, hoping to extract crucial information.
The suspect will face the third degree when they are brought in for questioning.
The investigators are currently applying the third degree to obtain a confession.
The officers were intensively interrogating the suspect, giving them the third degree throughout the night.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who is imprisoned or serving time in prison

examples.x: The notorious criminal is currently behind bars, serving a life sentence for his crimes.
He spent several years behind bars before finally being released on parole.
If convicted, the defendant will likely find himself behind bars for a substantial period of time.
The police are actively working to put the dangerous gang members behind bars.
He regrets the choices that led him to a life behind bars.
By this time next year, the corrupt politician will be facing justice and could potentially end up behind bars.
After his conviction, he found himself behind bars, facing a lengthy sentence.

senses.definition: to be in risk of receiving punishment or getting into trouble

examples.x: My brother is in for stealing a car.

senses.definition: strictly adhering to established rules, procedures, or standardized practices

examples.x: The detective solved the case by the book, meticulously gathering evidence and following every procedure.
The teacher graded the exams by the book, using the established criteria and marking scheme.
The jury deliberated carefully, ensuring that their verdict was reached by the book, considering the evidence and applicable laws.
The project manager emphasized the importance of completing the project by the book, adhering to the agreed-upon timeline and deliverables.

senses.definition: rules that determine what one should or should not do in a particular situation

examples.x: The employee should follow the dos and don'ts outlined in the company handbook.
The teacher emphasizes the dos and don'ts of classroom behavior to maintain a productive learning environment.
Remember to review the dos and don'ts of online etiquette before engaging in social media interactions.
The team carefully followed the dos and don'ts during the project to ensure its success.
The workshop will cover the dos and don'ts of effective time management.
We adhered to the dos and don'ts of camping to minimize our impact on the environment.
I will research the dos and don'ts of healthy eating to improve my diet.
If you want to succeed in this role, you must follow the dos and don'ts of professional conduct.
The manager gives the green light for the project to proceed.
The committee discusses whether to give the green light to the proposed changes.
They received the green light to start filming the movie last week.
The team will eagerly await the green light to begin implementing their innovative ideas.
If the budget is approved, we can expect the green light for hiring new employees.

senses.definition: a method or principle solely based on experience, which may not be completely accurate

examples.x: The rule of thumb suggests that you should save at least 10% of your income.
The rule of thumb states that a 20-minute power nap can boost productivity.
He used the rule of thumb to estimate the project's timeline.
Tomorrow, I will use the rule of thumb to estimate the cost of the renovation project.
The team will apply the rule of thumb to assess the feasibility of the new business venture.

senses.definition: used to describe people who are easily targeted for criticism or are attracting significant negative attention

examples.x: The journalist has several high-profile politicians in her crosshairs for an upcoming investigative report.
The controversial CEO had multiple regulatory agencies in his crosshairs during his tenure.
The investigative journalist targeted the corrupt organization in her crosshairs, revealing their illicit activities.

senses.definition: used to admit that the accusations or charges against one are true
a statement indicating that something is safe and free of any defects or problems

examples.x: Despite accusations that the company was responsible for polluting the lake, it received a clean bill of health from the environmental agency after a thorough investigation.
The building passed the safety inspection and received a clean bill of health, indicating that it was safe for occupancy
The mechanic gave the car a clean bill of health, saying it was in excellent condition.
The school's infrastructure was evaluated and given a clean bill of health, with no major structural or safety issues found
